title: Blonde
verdictLabel: Song of the year potential — twice over
ratingVerdict: A near-perfect mood piece that rewards patience over and over. Not background music — give it your full attention and it gives it back.
label: Boys Don't Cry
intro: Ten years of living with this record and it still refuses to sit still. Every time I think I've mapped it, a song I'd filed under skip cracks open and becomes the whole point of the week.
paragraphs: It's an album built out of restraint — half-finished thoughts, guitars that never quite resolve, vocals pitched up until they sound like someone else remembering you. The first few listens feel like overhearing a conversation through a wall. Then one day the wall isn't there and you're inside it.

What keeps me coming back isn't any single hook — it's the negative space. The way a track ends a beat before you're ready. The silence is load-bearing here, and almost nobody else builds it this carefully.
pullQuote: A record that sounds like it was mixed at 3 a.m. for an audience of one — and somehow that one is always you.

standoutTracks.title: Ivy
standoutTracks.review: The opening line does more emotional damage than most full albums. Nostalgia and regret holding hands.

standoutTracks.title: Self Control
standoutTracks.review: That outro is the closest music gets to an ache you can press play on. I let it loop more than I'll admit.

standoutTracks.title: Nights
standoutTracks.review: The beat switch is the most famous 40 seconds on the record for a reason. The whole album pivots on it.

standoutTracks.title: White Ferrari
standoutTracks.review: Devastatingly quiet. The kind of song you only play when you actually want to feel something.
